title: Today's Markets Brief: Paccar stock rises after senators propose ending truck tax
summary: A quick look at the day's market news, with Paccar stock on the rise and BlackRock's Rick Rieder on the AI bull market.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and this is your markets brief. First up, Paccar stock is on the move after senators proposed ending a tax on trucks. Now, I know what you're thinking - what's the big deal about truck taxes? Well, it turns out that this tax has been a bit of a burden on the industry, and if it's scrapped, it could mean more cash in the pockets of truck manufacturers like Paccar. According to Investing.com, the stock rose on the news, which is a pretty clear sign that investors think this is a good idea.

Next, I've got my eye on BlackRock's Rick Rieder, who's sounding pretty optimistic about the AI bull market. In an interview with CNBC, Rieder said he feels 'a bit more relaxed' about this AI-driven market than he did during the dotcom era. What's behind his confidence? Earnings growth and cash to be reinvested in stocks, apparently. I have to say, it's always interesting to hear from one of the biggest players in the investing world.

Now, I'm not one to read too much into individual opinions, but it's clear that Rieder thinks there's still some steam left in this AI market. And who knows? Maybe he's onto something. I mean, AI is changing the game in some pretty fundamental ways, so it's not entirely surprising that investors are excited about it.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
